calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: MECH-457 H-retention-auxiliary-decay: BC-auxiliary persistence sweep and competence half-life (trajectory DV)
items.note: H-retention-auxiliary-decay: BC-install raw_view, then sweep bc_aux_coef PERSISTENCE (constant 0.5 / annealed 0.5->0.0 / off) and measure the competence HALF-LIFE, a trajectory statistic by construction. Tests whether the prior is not HELD rather than not PROTECTED. Uses linear_anneal, NOT warm_then_anneal: the latter is parameterised by the shared warm_start_fraction, which would couple BC persistence to the EXPLORATION anneal and confound the single intervention; the schedule is deliberately OUTSIDE the mode-gate. Verified: the three arms' config slices differ in the bc_aux triple and nothing else, with use_distributional_critic False on all three. Guards the substrate's documented schedule-guard alias (an annealed arm passes bc_aux_coef=0.0 with a nonzero schedule, so a guard reading the constant would yield an OFF arm labelled ANNEALED) by asserting the realised bc_aux_coef_first/_last actually moved. Declared null: half-life is invariant to the auxiliary schedule -> the prior is NOT being out-competed by the RL objective. Motivating evidence: V3-EXQ-780 ran a constant bc_aux_coef=0.5 throughout and still lost 20.933 -> 11.667, so a persistent-but-fixed auxiliary is insufficient and the schedule dimension is untested. GOV-FANOUT-1 PAIR with V3-EXQ-788 -- queued together, never singly: adjudicating a retention leg in isolation is how a confident-but-wrong elimination enters the frozen ledger (the 780/781/782 cluster is the validating instance). Pre-registered in hypothesis_space_registry.v1.json question 'competence_floor'. Substrate gate MET 2026-07-19: mech457_distributional_critic (ree-v3 8e88ffc), mech457_bc_aux_schedule (9a8dbae) and mech457_retention_trajectory_probe (7e4f6e9) all status=implemented. Re-derive brake does NOT fire: the producer-half autopsy failure_autopsy_MECH-457-gov-fanout-1-cluster-780-781-782_2026-07-18.json records re_derive_brake.fired=false on four grounds (different question, different null, different measurement, and a positive control at 20.933 that no braked autopsy possessed). GOV-REUSE-1 checked: decisive readout is the post-installation competence TRAJECTORY; runs 780/781/782 all record competence_trajectory absent (terminal-only), so it is NOT recoverable by reanalysis -> run. Reference build is the NON-REGRESSED 128-wide / 3x budget / z_world detached / credit-replay 3 / topk 32, NOT the 769-falsified 256/5x. raw_view only (780: post-BC 20.933 with 3/3 seeds taking, vs 0.583 with 0/3 on z_world). Honours the four registry measurement_requirement constraints verbatim: (1) records the competence TRAJECTORY via the probe hook, not terminal competence; (2) the grid enumerates a 'manipulation succeeded and then decayed' branch; (3) routes on the declared covariate post_bc_foraging_competence, not the terminal criterion alone (780's grid enumerated only a ~0 null and so scored a successful manipulation as a null); (4) an install that did not take self-routes substrate_not_ready_requeue, never a retention verdict. Shared OFF cell mints reuse-eligible with include_driver_script_in_hash=False; the two legs' OFF fingerprints were verified IDENTICAL across drivers, so whichever runs first mints and the other reuses (no separate mint job queued -- neither sanctioned exception applies). Queue-only: promotes and demotes nothing; MECH-457 stays candidate/v3_pending.

items.title: P-comp: MECH-448 demotion x MECH-449 Go/No-Go composition at committed-class entropy (2x2 diagnostic; 699 re-run for adjudicable preconditions)
items.note: RE-RUN of V3-EXQ-699 for a CLEANLY ADJUDICABLE manifest. The shipped run (v3_exq_699_pcomp_demotion_x_gonogo_composition_20260623T053755Z_v3) is a sound PASS (interpretation_label levers_compound) that the REE_assembly indexer adjudicates `precondition_unmet` from a DECLARATION bug alone -- the science is unaffected. The gapa_consumed_summary_divergence_all_arms entry reported min(consumed_summary_pairwise_dist_mean)=0.004852 against a 0.05 continuous floor while its `met` was a COUNT-of-seeds predicate (c1b_holds = _maj_all(gapa_divergence), i.e. >=2 of 3 seeds divergent in EVERY arm). min-over-cells is strictly HARSHER than 'a majority of seeds': the run's own per-seed data has 11 of 12 arm-seeds divergent (only ARM_DEM/seed 43 below floor), so the author's met=True was correct and the reported statistic was the wrong aggregator. Driver corrected in ree-v3 0bfbb42 (_min_arm_count; entries now report min(per-arm satisfying-seed count) >= MIN_SEEDS_FOR_PASS, reproducing all(_maj(...)) exactly) -- PROSPECTIVE only, so it cannot fix the existing manifest. WHY RE-RUN NOT MANIFEST-EDIT: the indexer scores the PACK copy (<experiment_type>/runs/<run_id>/manifest.json, build_experiment_indexes.py:868); the flat <run_id>.json is an overlay restricted to _FLAT_AUTHORITATIVE_FIELDS, which deliberately excludes interpretation.preconditions -- so a flat edit is inert and a pack edit would rewrite the historical scoring source. Prior manifest is SUPERSEDED, not corrected. GOV-REUSE-1: the decisive readout (committed-class entropy C2 + the 7 readiness preconditions) IS recorded in the 20260623 manifest, but it is unusable as-adjudicated for the reason above, and correcting it in place is the rejected retroactive-edit path -- so this is a re-run, not a reanalysis. Design UNCHANGED from 699 (2x2 demotion {OFF,ON} x Go/No-Go {OFF,ON}, matched stack, 3 seeds x 4 arms, 350 ep/run); only QUEUE_ID and a new manifest `supersedes` field differ. Re-derive brake CLEAR (MECH-448 and MECH-449 each have 0 substrate_ceiling/non_contributory autopsies). experiment_purpose=diagnostic; PROMOTES NOTHING, scoring-excluded. Priority 5 = below the live MECH-463 probes (787 pri=60, 785b pri=6): the scientific answer is already known, only the record needs repair, so it should run on idle capacity. KNOWN CAVEAT (pre-existing, NOT introduced here, deliberately NOT fixed): validate_experiments flags stale-E3-diagnostics -- the driver reads last_score_diagnostics per env-step (line ~945) without clearing the latch, and with the default heartbeat.e3_steps_per_tick=10 each genuine E3 selection is re-recorded ~10x. The load-bearing readiness gates are RATIOS and MEANS (demotion_active_frac, excluded_count_mean, nogo_suppressed_mean, rank_preserving_frac), which are invariant under uniform replication, so the PASS is not threatened; what IS inflated is the reported tick COUNTS (n_p2_ticks, f_eligibility_demotion_active_ticks, go_nogo_active_ticks) as sample sizes. Fixing the latch would change the statistics and make this a DIFFERENT experiment (new number, not a letter), defeating the purpose of a faithful re-emission -- so it is left as-is and flagged for a separate follow-up. validate_experiments --strict OK; validate_recording --strict OK on the dry-run manifest (always-core complete, queue_id=V3-EXQ-699a, supersedes set); dry-run smoke PASS (4 arms end-to-end, 12 verdict lines at full scale, toy-scale self-routes substrate_not_ready_requeue as designed).
